questions.prompt: Why UUIDs as primary keys for user-facing tables in Supabase?

choices.text: Generated client-side (no server round-trip needed to know the ID), no information leakage (incrementing integers expose row counts), distributed-friendly (no collisions across regions or shards), and compatible with Supabase Auth's auth.users.id type. Default with `gen_random_uuid()` from pgcrypto. Exception: high-volume internal tables where bigint sequential IDs save storage and index size — but for user-facing data, UUIDs are the right default.

questions.explanation: Client-generation, no leakage, distributed-safe, matches auth.users.id.

questions.prompt: Designing a `posts` table that needs a status field (draft, published, archived). Enum or lookup table?

choices.text: Use a Postgres enum (`create type post_status as enum (...)`). The set is stable, the values are type-safe and indexable, and the schema documents the allowed values explicitly. Lookup tables shine when values evolve frequently (tag categories, plan tiers that change with business decisions) — for those, the join cost is worth the flexibility. Status codes for posts are set-in-stone; enum is the cleaner choice.

questions.explanation: Enum for stable sets; lookup table for evolving sets.
